title: Campaign is live and running
template: Hey! Yes, [Campaign] is live and running. Everything looks good on our end — impressions are flowing and the budget is pacing normally. Shout if you want [Numbers] broken out.

title: Campaign paused — with reason
template: Quick update: we paused [Campaign] because [reason — e.g. landing page issue, client request, budget cap]. Happy to walk through timing to turn it back on — just say the word.

title: Scheduled launch with date
template:
Hi [Name],

[Campaign] is scheduled to go live on [Date]. I’ll keep an eye on launch-day pacing and flag anything odd right away.

Best,
David

title: All campaigns overview
template: Here’s the snapshot across [Client]’s paid search: live campaigns are serving, nothing erroring out in the account, and we’re watching pacing daily. If you want a deeper dive on any one line, tell me which and I’ll pull [Numbers].

title: Just enabled or changed
template: Done — [Campaign] is enabled / updated as discussed. Changes can take a little bit to fully reflect in the UI, but you should see traffic pick up shortly. I’ll check back in a few hours to confirm it’s behaving.

title: Live status — email
template:
Hi [Name],

Yes — [Campaign] is currently live and running. Delivery and pacing look healthy on our side. I’m happy to share specific metrics or a quick Loom if that’s easier.

Best,
David

title: Budget pacing on track
template: Budget’s pacing right where we want it for [Campaign] — no fire drills. I’ll keep monitoring, especially if volume shifts, and ping you if anything looks off.

title: Underspending — context
template: We’re a bit light on spend for [Campaign] right now — usually a mix of auction softness, bid caps, or audience overlap. I’m already testing a few levers to pull volume up without wrecking efficiency. Will update once we have a clearer read.

title: Overspending — need approval
template:
Hi [Name],

We’re pacing hot on [Campaign] (roughly [Amount] ahead of where we’d expect for this point in the period). I can pull levers to slow it down, or — if you’re comfortable — we can request a budget bump so we don’t throttle winners. Let me know which direction you prefer and I’ll move today.

Best,
David

title: Budget change acknowledged
template: Got it — noted the budget change to [Amount] for [Campaign]. I’ll implement, double-check delivery after the change, and follow up if anything looks funky in the first 24 hours.

title: Performance is strong
template: [Campaign] is in a good spot — we’re seeing strong efficiency vs. recent baseline and the query mix looks cleaner than it has in a while. I’ll still keep an eye on anything that spikes CPAs, but you’re in a solid window right now.

title: Needs improvement — plan
template: Fair flag — performance on [Campaign] has softened. Here’s the plan I’m executing: tighten loose match types / negatives where waste showed up, refresh ad copy on the fatigued assets, and re-check geo/device bid adjustments. I’ll report back with [Numbers] once those changes have a few days to settle.

title: Monthly performance summary
template:
Hi [Name],

Quick monthly recap for [Client]: we held or improved core efficiency metrics on [Campaign] vs. last month, with the main story being [Numbers / one-line narrative]. I’ve attached the detail; happy to walk through anything that jumps out on our next call.

Best,
David

title: Quick metrics snapshot
template: Snapshot for [Campaign] (last 7d vs. prior 7d): [Numbers]. TL;DR — [one line: e.g. CPA flat, conv volume up]. Ask if you want this sliced by brand/non-brand or device.

title: Promo active and serving
template: The promo extension / sitelink for [Campaign] is approved and live — it’s showing in the wild. If you want screenshots or a quick check on which ad groups it’s attached to, I can send that over.

title: Promo disapproved — fixing
template: Heads up: Google disapproved the promo asset for [Campaign] — usually policy wording or a date mismatch. I’m revising copy / dates to match their rules and will resubmit today; turnaround is often same-day once it’s clean.

title: New promo request acknowledged
template:
Hi [Name],

Thanks for the promo details — I’ve got the request for [Campaign] and will build the extensions / sitelinks, run them through policy best practices, and send a preview before pushing live.

Best,
David

title: Report will be sent on day
template:
Hi [Name],

You’ll have the [Client] paid search report in your inbox on [day] — same sections as last time unless you want anything added. If there’s a specific question leadership will ask, send it over and I’ll front-load the answer.

Best,
David

title: Report attached / just sent
template: Just sent over the report for [Client] / [Campaign] — [Numbers] are in the summary tab if you only have two minutes. Happy to jump on a quick sync if anything needs context.

title: Meeting prep — talking points
template: For the call: (1) [Campaign] pacing + any budget watchouts, (2) performance vs. goal — [Numbers], (3) what we’re testing next / what we need from you (creative, promo dates, LP). I can drive slides or keep it conversational — your call.

title: Weekly recap intro
template: Weekly recap for [Client]: overall spend and conversions tracked about where we expected; the main movers were [Numbers / narrative]. Deeper detail is in the deck — flag anything you want expanded for leadership.

title: Back by EOD
template: On it — I’ll get you a solid answer by EOD today. If anything blocking pops up earlier, I’ll ping you before then.

title: In meetings — reply tonight
template: Swamped in meetings today but I saw this — I’ll dig in and reply tonight. If it’s truly urgent (revenue down / ads off), text me and I’ll prioritize.

title: Handling now — update soon
template: Got it — I’m on this now and will send an update shortly with what I changed / what I’m seeing in the account.

title: New client onboarding intro
template:
Hi [Name],

I’m David — I’ll be your paid search lead on the Hawke side. I’ve already done a first pass on the account and have a short list of quick wins vs. longer-term bets; we can align on goals, tracking, and reporting rhythm on our kickoff. Looking forward to working with you.

Best,
David

title: Campaign launch confirmation
template:
Hi [Name],

[Campaign] is officially live as of [Date]. Initial delivery looks normal; I’ll monitor closely over the next 48 hours and share a short launch readout once we have enough data to mean something.

Best,
David

title: Optimization recommendations
template: Here are the paid search recommendations I’d prioritize for [Client]: (1) [topic / e.g. search terms & negatives], (2) [topic / e.g. RSA refresh & testing], (3) [topic / e.g. bid strategy or budget reallocation]. None of these are “because Google said so” — they’re tied to what we’re seeing in [Numbers]. Happy to implement whatever you green-light first.

title: Quarter-end review summary
template:
Hi [Name],

Quarter wrap for [Client] paid search: we [one-line outcome vs. goal], with the biggest learnings around [Numbers / narrative]. I’ve attached the summary; if you want a tighter version for execs, I can cut a one-pager.

Best,
David

title: Task acknowledged — on it
template: Seen — I’m on it. Will circle back when it’s done or if I hit a blocker.

title: Need input on topic
template: Quick ask — I need your input on [topic] before I move forward on [Client] / [Campaign]. What’s your take / who owns the final call?

title: Handoff to person
template: Looping in [person] — they’re closer to [topic] on this one and can get you an answer faster than I can from the search side. I’ll stay available if anything touches bidding or account structure.

title: Pacing check-in — Slack
template: Pacing check for [Campaign]: we’re on track for the month against [Amount]. No action needed unless you want to push harder — then we can talk bid/budget.

title: Performance email — neutral update
template:
Hi [Name],

Sharing a quick performance update on [Campaign]: results this period are [Numbers vs. goal]. I’m including context on what drove the shift and what we’re doing next so it’s not just a dashboard screenshot.

Best,
David

title: Paused — email to client
template:
Hi [Name],

I wanted to let you know we’ve paused [Campaign] as of [Date] because [reason]. I’m happy to discuss timing to resume or any adjustments you’d like on the go-live plan.

Best,
David

title: Report delay — honest
template:
Hi [Name],

Running a little behind on the report — you’ll have it by [Date]. The numbers are pulled; I’m tightening the narrative so it’s actually useful, not just export-and-send.

Best,
David

title: Thanks + next steps — both
template: Thanks for the context — super helpful. Next steps on my side: I’ll [action] for [Campaign] and follow up by [Date] with [Numbers] or a short summary, whichever is more useful.
